title: macOS and iOS Support, Security, and Recovery · Support Retrieval
summary: A delayed incident review reconstructs macos and ios support, security, and recovery from user impact, asset identity, safety, baseline, symptom, recent change, hypothesis, test, observation, repair, verification, prevention, escalation, documentation, and transfer evidence. Planned artifact: Extend the evidence record for Apple platform support and recovery record after a delayed changed case. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and e…
objectives: Distinguish Apple hardware architecture, macOS and iOS roles, accounts, Apple Account, local users, managed identities, services, keychain, and device-management boundaries.
Use Finder, System Settings, Activity Monitor, Disk Utility, System Information, Terminal, logs, safe mode, recovery, and accessibility tools for bounded evidence.
Trace APFS volumes, snapshots, encryption, free space, iCloud state, local optimization, versions, conflicts, backup, and restore without assuming sync equals backup.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.config:
# comptia-a-plus cumulative fictional support evidence portfolio
# Browser labs perform deterministic text-contract review only. They never execute learner
# commands, inspect the host, touch devices or accounts, connect to networks, open equipment,
# handle malware, erase media, contact people, or claim certification.
workspace: support
ticket-id: fictional-ticket
user-impact: [replace with user outcome, scope, severity, and urgency]
environment: [replace with asset, version, configuration, owner, dependency, and evidence time]
safety-authority: [replace with consent, hazards, data custody, privacy, access, and stop rules]
baseline: [replace with expected behavior, known-good state, conditions, and measurement]
symptom: [replace with observed behavior, reproduction, frequency, timing, and evidence]
recent-change: [replace with timeline, relevant changes, counterevidence, and unknowns]
hypothesis: [replace with falsifiable cause, competing explanation, prediction, and confidence]
test: [replace with one safe reversible discriminating test, changed case, and stop rule]
observation: [replace with actual result, provenance, rejected inference, and next decision]
repair-rollback: [replace with least-change repair, authority, backup, trigger, and restored state]
verify: [replace with user outcome, original, changed, side-effect, restart, and recurrence checks]
prevention: [replace with prevention, monitoring, owner, review date, and residual risk]
escalation: [replace with stop threshold, destination, evidence packet, and requested decision]
documentation: [replace with plain structured status, redaction, access, handoff, and uncertainty]
transfer-boundary: authorized disposable equipment or simulator only
review:

stimulus.title: Apple Changed Case case record
stimulus.caption: Three excerpts from a learner evidence packet; only one connects a claim to a reproducible check.

lines.label: assumption
lines.text: Mixed Apple silicon and Intel devices have sync conflicts, trust prompts, full storage, managed policy, encrypted recovery, and peripheral failures. The review variation 5 changes the user outcome, device, version, hazard, recent change, symptom, access need, competing cause, and transfer limit. Competency probe: Verify boot, sleep, power, storage, app, account, peripheral, network, accessibility, backup, recovery, and managed-policy behavior after a least-change repair.

lines.label: risk
lines.text: A repair that works on one unmanaged Mac transfers unchanged to every managed Apple device.

lines.label: evidence
lines.text: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Verify boot, sleep, power, storage, app, account, peripheral, network, accessibility, backup, recovery, and managed-policy behavior after a least-change repair. Artifact under review: Apple platform support and recovery record.

options.text: The record names the assumption, applies Apple Changed Case, and reports a changed-case check tied to the stakeholder decision.
The record says the result looks right but supplies no quantity, trace, condition, or counterexample. That claim fails the evidence boundary for Apple Changed Case.

For separate evidence from confidence, apply one familiar repair, omit user impact, authorization, safety, asset and version identity, baseline, recent change, competing cause, prediction, observation, rollback, changed-case verification, accessibility, privacy, escalation, and controlled transfer, then accept a missing symptom as support proof.
